key_insight: A general-purpose ACE interatomic potential can reproduce niobium’s diverse physics with near-DFT accuracy across local structures and extreme conditions.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a fast, scalable model for simulating niobium in applications involving defects, high pressure, phonons, and fracture without relying on full DFT.
method: Trained an atomic cluster expansion potential on thousands of DFT niobium structures spanning many local environments.
Validated performance against phonons, high-pressure response, and dislocation-glide energy barriers.
Compared the ACE with empirical and machine-learning potentials, including a new universal ML model.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports training on thousands of DFT structures and validation through broad property tests, including a near-million-atom fracture molecular dynamics simulation.
watch_item: Performance may depend on whether new simulations stay within the structural and thermodynamic regimes represented in the DFT training set.
paper_title: A general-purpose atomic cluster expansion interatomic potential for niobium
